prose:
Method–provenance summary  
Property: Fukui functions (f+, f−, dual descriptor) and most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites for benzaldehyde.  
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF.  
Geometry provenance: single-point evaluation on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization; notation: DFT//input-XYZ).  
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).  
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).  
Software: chemkit (via PySCF backend).  
Convergence & integrity: integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.  
Warnings (verbatim from result JSON):  
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• common header present  
Live log: the calculation was executed via chemkit; progress was streamed to the automatically generated fukui_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory (available for inspection).  

Headline result  
The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C) with f⁺ = 0.39938963.  
The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.23839906.  
Full per-atom f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor values and the generated bar-chart PNG are available in /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp3kt0_fm5/live.json and the path given above.
